---
title: "Artificial Intelligence (AI) - The Basics and Use Cases"
format: pptx
---

## Module 1: Artificial Intelligence (AI) - The Basics and Use Cases (AIM Training)


## Learning Objectives

- Understand AI Fundamentals
- Distinguish AI Types
- Articulate Business Value
- Identify Functional Use Cases
- Evaluate Business Opportunities

## Understanding AI Fundamentals

- Definition: Simulating human intelligence in machines.
- Practical Example: An automated email sorter that categorizes incoming customer inquiries.

## Distinguishing AI Types

- Machine Learning (ML): Product recommendations on e-commerce sites.
- Deep Learning (HTML): Advanced facial recognition.
- Natural Language Processing (NLP): Chatbots/Virtual Assistants (Siri/Alexa).
- Computer Vision (CV): Automated quality inspection in manufacturing.
- Generative AI (GenAI): Creating marketing copy or images using tools like ChatGPT or Midjourney.

## Articulating Business Value

- Increasing Efficiency: Automation of repetitive tasks.
- Improving Decision-Making: Data-driven insights.
- Enhancing Customer Experience: 24/7 support.
- Cost Reduction: Optimizing resources.
- Competitive Advantage: Staying ahead of market trends.
- Practical Example: Using predictive analytics to manage inventory levels, reducing waste and costs.

## Identifying Functional Use Cases

- Customer Service: Chatbots for FAQ handling.
- Marketing: Personalized email campaigns and targeted ads.
- Operations: Predictive maintenance for machinery.
- Finance: Fraud detection and automated invoicing.
- Practical Example: A retail business using AI to send personalized discount codes to customers based on past purchases.

## Evaluating Business Opportunities

- Process for Assessment:
- 1. Identify pain points.
- 2. Evaluate data availability.
- 3. Pilot small.
- Practical Example: A small bakery using a simple AI tool to predict daily demand based on weather and local events.

## Summary

- Recap of Learning Objectives:
- - Understand AI Fundamentals
- - Distinguish AI Types
- - Articulate Business Value
- - Identify Functional Use Cases
- - Evaluate Business Opportunities
